dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic narrowly led the day: HN commenters mostly defended Mythos against a small-model critique and gave Claude a smaller coding-quality nod, which outweighed one skeptical benchmark comment [E1][E3][E5].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic and OpenAI both had mixed-but-net-positive HN signal, but Anthropic's positive coverage felt slightly stronger. The biggest AI discussion defended Anthropic's Mythos methodology rather than dunking on it, and Claude picked up an additional small competence win in a separate dev-tools thread [E1][E5]. OpenAI also got capability praise for GPT/Codex, but its clearest standalone company story was the Cirrus Labs acquihire, where commenters focused on Cirrus CI shutting down and the downside for open-source users [E2][E4][E6].

openai.judgementSnippet: Mixed to mildly positive: GPT/Codex received capability praise, but the Cirrus Labs acquisition thread leaned negative because commenters focused on Cirrus CI shutting down and related open-source concerns [E2][E4][E6].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Mildly positive: commenters largely defended Anthropic's Mythos claims in the main security thread and praised Claude in a smaller coding example, though one benchmark thread included a skeptical Mythos aside [E1][E3][E5].

evidence.summary: In the Mythos comparison thread, several top comments say the small-model blog post changes the task by isolating vulnerable code, and defend Anthropic's full-codebase/frontier-model approach; one commenter also says Opus 4.6 works well on this kind of review.
The same Mythos thread gives GPT 5.4 a positive side mention, with commenters saying frontier models like GPT 5.4 succeed on bug-finding tasks where weaker models do not.
In the benchmark-exploit thread, a top comment speculates Anthropic has not released Mythos because the product might disappoint relative to benchmark scores.
The Cirrus Labs/OpenAI thread frames the move as an acquihire that will shut down Cirrus CI, with commenters raising open-source project concerns and regretting the loss of the service.
In the git diff driver thread, a top comment says Claude one-shotted the integration portion of implementing a diff driver.
In the browser game Show HN thread, a top comment reports Codex 5.4 figured out the game and wrote an auto-play script that reached the current high score.
